rulings.Case Title: KEVIN NEALAN VS. LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Expert Witness Disclosure
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 9, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT LEDCOR CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Notice Of Motion And Motion To Augment Expert Witness Disclosure. The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Defendant Ledcor Construction, Inc.'s motion to augment expert list is denied on the grounds that defendant did not timely file the motion and plaintiff would be unduly prejudiced by allowing a late designation at this point. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 2034.620(b) [court shall grant leave to augment only if "[t]he court has determined that any party opposing the motion will not be prejudiced in maintaining that party's action or defense on the merits"]; id. § 2034.620(c) [requiring showing of "reasonable diligence" or, in case of failure to offer expert earlier due as a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect, that moving party "promptly" seek leave to augment].) Indeed, the motion was not made early enough to permit the deposition of the proposed new expert to be taken within 15 days before trial, and defendant has not made any showing of "exceptional circumstances." (Id. § 2034.610(b).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: PAMELA SNYDER VS. FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidence Sanctions, Issue Sanctins, Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pamela Snyder
rulings.Rulings: Sept. 9, 2019 Line 1, DEFENDANT FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion For Evidence Sanctions, Issue Sanctins, Terminating Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pamela Snyder The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part, with supplemental briefing and a continued hearing required: Plaintiff is in violation of the Court's October 16, 2018 Order Re Discovery (Ex A to Moss Declaration). Plaintiff, in pro per, is advised that the prospect of settlement, while perhaps desirable, in no way affects the trial date or her obligations as a litigant to respond under oath in writing to written discovery requests. Plaintiff reports, and defendant confirms, that many documents have been produced by Plaintiff in or about late November 2018, perhaps as many as 1,000.00. No specifics are offered by the moving party whether these documents were responsive to the subject discovery requests or not. Plaintiff nevertheless is presently in violation of her obligations as a litigant and is in violation of the Court's October 16, 2018 Order at least with respect to the other written discovery that are the subject of the October 2018 Order and good cause therefore exists to grant some of the requested relief. However, because the actual discovery items that are the subject of the Oct. 2018 Order were not provided on this motion, this Court cannot recite any particular issue it might deem precluded as a sanction, nor any particular evidence it might conclude should be excluded as a sanction. The moving party is ordered to file a supplemental brief or declaration offering the discovery at issue upon which the Court may base an evidentiary or issue sanction and this hearing shall be continued for about one week for the purpose of providing that supplemental briefing to the Court; if Plaintiff fails to provide verified responses to all the discovery mentioned in the October 16, 2018 Order, without objections, by the date of that continued hearing, then it is the Court's intention to grant appropriate issue or evidentiary sanctions as requested. In the court's discretion, a terminating sanction will not be granted. Monetary sanctions of $1,665.00, or as required by the supplemental briefing, are to be awarded on the final hearing of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302).

rulings.Case Title: ANNA GATTI VS. LOOP AI LAB INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Appearance By Anna Gatti On A Date Certain In San Francisco
rulings.Rulings: Sept. 9, 2019 Line 2, DEFENDANTS LOOP AI LABS INC., GIANMAURO CALAFIORE's Motion To Compel Appearance By Anna Gatti On A Date Certain In San Francisco Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. The August 13, 2018 deposition was reasonably taken off calendar based on pro per plaintiff's written representations she could not appear. While the Court generally prefers for the parties to resolve discovery disputes informally and demonstrate genuine meet and confer on the discovery issue in question, here, given the circumstances described in the moving papers relating to prior efforts to accomplish the deposition in Italy or in San Francisco, and prior failures to voluntarily comply, good cause exists to grant the requested relief. Plaintiff's papers indicate her agreement to the September 16 deposition date. Good cause appearing, she is ordered to appear for deposition on September 16 at Barklay Reporting Services 201 California Street Suit 375 San Francisco, deposition to commence at 10:00 a.m. The Request for a Discovery Master must be made by separate motion, not via reply briefing on this motion, and that request is therefore denied without prejudice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (JPT/302)

If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARIO BUBBO VS. ANGEL ISLAND - TIBURON FERRY, INC. A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Filed By Angel Island - Tiburon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, September 9, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT ANGEL ISLAND - TIBURON FERRY, INC.'s AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Angel Island - Tiburon Ferry, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. "Because a summary judgment denies the adversary party a trial, it should be granted with caution. [Citation.] Declarations of the moving party are strictly construed, those of the opposing party are liberally construed, and doubts as to whether a summary judgment should be granted must be resolved in favor of the opposing party." (Carrera v. Maurice J. Sopp & Son (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 366, 377.) [The Court's full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
